claim: The citation for Chiri is missing. It is mentioned in the prose but not linked to a reference.

all_issues.issue: The citation for Chiri is missing. It is mentioned in the prose but not linked to a reference.
all_issues.quote: Kindaichi and Chiri <Ref k="kindaichi1936" /> through the modern reference grammars of Tamura <Ref k="tamura1996" />; <Ref k="tamura2000" />, Refsing

all_issues.issue: The text names Chiri alongside Kindaichi but only cites Kindaichi 1936. The table lists Chiri 1942 as a separate defining work, so Chiri should have its own citation here.
all_issues.quote: drawing on the full range of prior scholarship from Kindaichi and Chiri <Ref k="kindaichi1936" />

all_issues.issue: Chiri is mentioned but not cited with a reference key; only Kindaichi1936 is cited here, creating a citation mismatch.

claim: The prose mentions Tamura's Saru dictionary and grammar monograph, but only cites the dictionary (<Ref k="tamura1996" />) and then the grammar monograph (<Ref k="tamura2000" />) separately. It might be clearer to link both to the description of the Saru dialect.

all_issues.issue: The prose mentions Tamura's Saru dictionary and grammar monograph, but only cites the dictionary (<Ref k="tamura1996" />) and then the grammar monograph (<Ref k="tamura2000" />) separately. It might be clearer to link both to the description of the Saru dialect.

The semicolon-separated citations are not formatted consistently with the rest of the text; typically, multiple citations are separated by commas or listed in a single <Ref> tag if supported. This may be a formatting error.

claim: The prose mentions Kindaichi and Chiri together as establishing basic morphological architecture, but Chiri is not cited here, only Kindaichi.

all_issues.issue: The prose mentions Kindaichi and Chiri together as establishing basic morphological architecture, but Chiri is not cited here, only Kindaichi.
The citation for Kindaichi 1936 is repeated at the end of the sentence, but it was already cited earlier in the paragraph. This creates redundant citation markup.
